resources.string: This screen shows navigation, declarative layout, and a modern look built with Jetpack Compose. The drawer and home screen demonstrate how to build a flexible native UI with Compose.
Open the drawer from the top-left icon to see how Compose handles navigation, visual hierarchy, and interactive surfaces.

The technical library is designed to show service consumption with Retrofit. Although the backend is currently mocked inside the app, the flow uses a real HTTP client, repository, and data parsing as if it talked to an external API.
From the home screen, tap the “Read articles” FAB to trigger the remote load and browse the content received by Retrofit.
The user CRUD exists to show local persistence with Room. Create, edit, list, and delete operations are stored in the device database and reappear when reopening the app.
Use “Create user”, “User list”, and “Edit active user” to go through the full Room-persisted flow.

The app integrates Jetpack Compose, Material 3, Navigation Compose, ViewModel, Coroutines, Flow, Hilt, Retrofit, OkHttp, Moshi, Room, Coil, Timber, Kotlinx Serialization, Compose Richtext, and LeakCanary, plus automated tests built with JUnit, kotlinx-coroutines-test, ActivityScenario, UiAutomator, Espresso, and Compose UI Test.
This step summarizes the full stack behind the UI, navigation, local data, mocked network layer, DI, observability, memory diagnostics, and automated validation.
UI: Compose, Material 3, and Navigation Compose
Data: Room, Retrofit, OkHttp, and Moshi
Support: Hilt, Flow, Coil, Timber, Serialization, and LeakCanary
Tests: JUnit, coroutines-test, ActivityScenario, UiAutomator, Espresso, and Compose UI Test
